title: (Sacramento, CA) Senator Bob Archuleta (D-Pico Rivera) has introduced legislation aimed at providing cost savings for water suppliers facing new water conservation mandates.
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem
raw_preview:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Two 2018 laws require urban retail water suppliers to reduce water use in residential and commercial sectors of the economy.",
    "The State Water Resources Control Board has prepared regulations to implement the laws known as 'Making Water Conservation a Way of Life.'"
raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Two 2018 laws require urban retail water suppliers to reduce water use in residential and commercial sectors of the economy.",
    "The State Water Resources Control Board has prepared regulations to implement the laws known as 'Making Water Conservation a Way of Life.'",
    "Water suppliers have expressed concern that compliance with the regulations will be costly and yield little in the way of water savings.",
    "The Legislative Analyst's Office (LAO) supports an effort to introduce legislation to provide suppliers more time and greater flexibility to meet the mandates.",
    "The LAO report noted that the long-term urban water use efficiency framework to 'Make Conservation a California Way of Life' creates new requirements for about 405 urban retail water suppliers.",
    "The LAO found that the State Water Board’s proposed regulations will create challenges for water suppliers in several key ways.",
    "The LAO concluded that the performance measures suppliers must implement for commercial customers are unnecessarily complex.",
    "The LAO reported that the performance measures for commercial customers lack clarity in places.",
    "The LAO stated that the performance measures will be administratively burdensome to implement.",
    "The LAO noted that the State Water Board proposes stringent standards for outdoor use, leaving suppliers with limited options for compliance.",
    "The new framework is estimated to result in cumulative costs in the low tens of billions of dollars from 2025 through 2040.",
    "These costs from the new framework will be borne primarily by suppliers, wastewater agencies, and customers."
  ]
}
```
